---
title: "county level"
format: html
---

# Create maps and scatterplots 
## mapping policies (cep rate and NSLP spending vs child well-being variables)
### IL map
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# =====================================================
# 1. File paths (adjust if needed)
# =====================================================

CEP_CSV  = r"..\data\derived-data\cep_county_IL.csv"
POV_CSV  = r"..\data\derived-data\child_pov_il.csv"
VOTE_CSV = r"..\data\derived-data\vote_2024_il.csv"

# =====================================================
# 2. Load datasets
# =====================================================
cep  = pd.read_csv(CEP_CSV)     # Must contain: county_fips, cep_school_rate
pov  = pd.read_csv(POV_CSV)     # Must contain: county_fips, poverty_rate_children
vote = pd.read_csv(VOTE_CSV)    # Must contain: county_fips, dem_margin

# Ensure county_fips is a 5-digit string
for df in (cep, pov, vote):
    df["county_fips"] = df["county_fips"].astype(str).str.zfill(5)
# Divide child pov rate by 100
pov["poverty_rate_children"] = pov["poverty_rate_children"] / 100

# =====================================================
# 3. Load Illinois county shapefile
# =====================================================
url = "https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/TIGER2024/COUNTY/tl_2024_us_county.zip"
counties = gpd.read_file(url)
gdf = counties.copy()

# Construct 5-digit county FIPS from TIGER columns
gdf["county_fips"] = (
    gdf["STATEFP"].astype(str).str.zfill(2) +
    gdf["COUNTYFP"].astype(str).str.zfill(3)
)

# Filter to Illinois only (STATEFP = 17)
gdf_il = gdf[gdf["STATEFP"].astype(str).str.zfill(2) == "17"].copy()

# Reproject for better map appearance
gdf_il = gdf_il.to_crs(epsg=5070)

# =====================================================
# 4. Merge county-level metrics
# =====================================================
df_il = (
    cep[["county_fips", "cep_school_rate"]]
    .merge(pov[["county_fips", "poverty_rate_children"]],
           on="county_fips", how="left")
    .merge(vote[["county_fips", "dem_margin"]],
           on="county_fips", how="left")
)

gdf_il = gdf_il.merge(df_il, on="county_fips", how="left")

# =====================================================
# 5. Mapping function (save as PNG for slides)
# =====================================================
def display_map(gdf, column, title,
                cmap="viridis",
                vmin=None, vmax=None,
                center_zero=False):

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8.5, 6))
    ax.set_axis_off()

    # Symmetric scale for political margin
    if center_zero:
        values = gdf[column].dropna()
        max_abs = float(np.nanmax(np.abs(values))) if len(values) else 1.0
        vmin, vmax = -max_abs, max_abs
        cmap = "RdBu"

    gdf.plot(
        column=column,
        ax=ax,
        cmap=cmap,
        vmin=vmin,
        vmax=vmax,
        legend=True,
        legend_kwds={"shrink": 0.75},
        edgecolor="white",
        linewidth=0.4
    )

    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=14)
    filename = title.replace(" ", "_").replace("(", "").replace(")", "")
    plt.savefig(f"../figures/maps/{filename}.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# =====================================================
# 6. Generate maps for presentation
# =====================================================

# Democratic margin (diverging scale centered at zero)
display_map(
    gdf_il,
    column="dem_margin",
    title="Illinois Counties: Democratic Margin (2024)",
    center_zero=True
)

# Child poverty rate
display_map(
    gdf_il,
    column="poverty_rate_children",
    title="Illinois Counties: Child Poverty Rate"
)

# CEP participation rate
display_map(
    gdf_il,
    column="cep_school_rate",
    title="Illinois Counties: CEP Participation Rate (Share of Schools)",
    vmin=0,
    vmax=1
)
```

### IL Scatter plot
```{python}
# Scatter plot
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Merge everything into one dataframe
df_analysis = (
    df_il[["county_fips", "dem_margin",
           "poverty_rate_children",
           "cep_school_rate"]]
)

sns.regplot(data=df_analysis,
                x="poverty_rate_children",
                y="cep_school_rate")
# correlation
x = "poverty_rate_children"
y = "cep_school_rate"
r = df_analysis[[x, y]].dropna().corr().iloc[0, 1]
plt.text(
    0.02, 0.98,
    f"r = {r:.3f}",
    transform=plt.gca().transAxes,
    ha="left", va="top",
    fontsize=12
)
plt.title("Child Poverty vs CEP Participation")
plt.savefig("../figures/scatterplots/child_pov_vs_cep_IL.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")

plt.show()

sns.regplot(data=df_analysis,
                x="dem_margin",
                y="cep_school_rate")
# correlation
x = "dem_margin"
y = "cep_school_rate"
r = df_analysis[[x, y]].dropna().corr().iloc[0, 1]
plt.text(
    0.02, 0.98,
    f"r = {r:.3f}",
    transform=plt.gca().transAxes,
    ha="left", va="top",
    fontsize=12
)

plt.title("Political Leaning vs CEP Participation")
plt.savefig("../figures/scatterplots/dem_margin_vs_cep_IL.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

```

### MO map
```{python}
# =====================================================
# 1. Load the datafile
# =====================================================
counties = gpd.read_file(url)
## Missouri = STATEFP 29
counties_mo = counties[counties["STATEFP"] == "29"].copy()
counties_mo.head()
# Load other data sets
cep  = pd.read_csv(CEP_CSV,  dtype={"county_fips": str})
pov  = pd.read_csv(POV_CSV,  dtype={"county_fips": str})
vote = pd.read_csv(VOTE_CSV, dtype={"county_fips": str})

for df in (cep, pov, vote):
    df["county_fips"] = (
        df["county_fips"]
        .str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)  
        .str.strip()                           
        .str.zfill(5)                          
    )

gdf = counties.copy()

# Construct 5-digit county FIPS from TIGER columns
gdf["county_fips"] = (
    gdf["STATEFP"].astype(str).str.zfill(2) +
    gdf["COUNTYFP"].astype(str).str.zfill(3)
)

# Filter to Illinois only (STATEFP = 17)
gdf_mo = gdf[gdf["STATEFP"].astype(str).str.zfill(2) == "29"].copy()

# Reproject for better map appearance
gdf_mo = gdf_mo.to_crs(epsg=5070)

# =====================================================
# 2. Merge county-level metrics
# =====================================================
df_mo = (
    pov[["county_fips", "poverty_rate_children"]]
      .merge(vote[["county_fips", "dem_margin"]], on="county_fips", how="left")
      .merge(cep[["county_fips", "cep_school_rate"]], on="county_fips", how="left")
)

gdf_mo = gdf_mo.merge(df_mo, on="county_fips", how="left")

# =====================================================
# 3. Mapping function (save as PNG for slides)
# =====================================================
def display_map(gdf, column, title,
                cmap="viridis",
                vmin=None, vmax=None,
                center_zero=False):

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8.5, 6))
    ax.set_axis_off()

    # Symmetric scale for political margin
    if center_zero:
        values = gdf[column].dropna()
        max_abs = float(np.nanmax(np.abs(values))) if len(values) else 1.0
        vmin, vmax = -max_abs, max_abs
        cmap = "RdBu"

    gdf.plot(
        column=column,
        ax=ax,
        cmap=cmap,
        vmin=vmin,
        vmax=vmax,
        legend=True,
        legend_kwds={"shrink": 0.75},
        edgecolor="white",
        linewidth=0.4
    )

    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=14
    filename = title.replace(" ", "_").replace("(", "").replace(")", "")
    plt.savefig(f"../figures/maps/{filename}.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight"))
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# =====================================================
# 5. Generate maps for presentation
# =====================================================

# Democratic margin (diverging scale centered at zero)
display_map(
    gdf_mo,
    column="dem_margin",
    title="Missouri Counties: Democratic Margin (2024)",
    center_zero=True
)

# Child poverty rate
display_map(
    gdf_mo,
    column="poverty_rate_children",
    title="Missouri Counties: Child Poverty Rate"
)

# CEP participation rate
display_map(
    gdf_mo,
    column="cep_school_rate",
    title="Missouri Counties: CEP Participation Rate (Share of Schools)",
    vmin=0,
    vmax=1
)
```

### MO map
```{python}
# Scatter plot
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Merge everything into one dataframe
df_analysis = (
    df_mo[["county_fips", "dem_margin",
           "poverty_rate_children",
           "cep_school_rate"]]
)

sns.regplot(data=df_analysis,
                x="poverty_rate_children",
                y="cep_school_rate")
# correlation
x = "poverty_rate_children"
y = "cep_school_rate"
r = df_analysis[[x, y]].dropna().corr().iloc[0, 1]
plt.text(
    0.02, 0.98,
    f"r = {r:.3f}",
    transform=plt.gca().transAxes,
    ha="left", va="top",
    fontsize=12
)
plt.title("Child Poverty vs CEP Participation")
plt.savefig("../figures/scatterplots/dem_margin_vs_child_pov.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

sns.regplot(data=df_analysis,
                x="dem_margin",
                y="cep_school_rate")
# correlation
y = "cep_school_rate"
x = "dem_margin"
r = df_analysis[[x, y]].dropna().corr().iloc[0, 1]
plt.text(
    0.02, 0.98,
    f"r = {r:.3f}",
    transform=plt.gca().transAxes,
    ha="left", va="top",
    fontsize=12
)
plt.title("Political Leaning vs CEP Participation")
plt.savefig("../figures/scatterplots/dem_margin_vs_cep_IL.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()


```

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("../data/raw-data/file.csv")
df.head()
```
